commit.message:
fix: add duration-based overlap check to /reserve endpoint

Previously /reserve only relied on Durable Object lock (same start+end)
and D1 UNIQUE constraint (same tenant+staff+start_at). A 60-min menu at
06:30 could be booked even though a 06:00-07:00 reservation existed for
the same staff — because start_at differed.

Now /reserve checks for duration-based overlap before INSERT:
  SELECT ... WHERE (staff_id = ? OR staff_id = 'any' OR staff_id IS NULL)
    AND start_at < endAt AND end_at > startAt

This matches the same logic as /slots bookableForMenu, making the API
the true source of truth. Returns 409 with error="duration_overlap" and
conflictWith details.

Also updated StepConfirm.tsx to handle duration_overlap as a slot
conflict (navigates back to date/time selection).

E2E verified on production:
- Test A: bookable slot 06:00 → OK (reservation created)
- Test B: not-bookable slot 11:15 (60min overlaps 12:00) → 409 duration_overlap
- Test C: same time on different tenant → OK (tenant isolation)
- Test D: specific staff overlap 06:30 (overlaps 06:00-07:00) → 409 duration_overlap

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(terminal): 修复生产环境 Ctrl+R 被拦截无法触发终端反向历史搜索 (#344)

生产环境下 electron-toolkit 的 optimizer 会通过 before-input-event
阻止 Ctrl+R 到达渲染进程。通过包装 optimizer 的监听器，在检测到
Ctrl+R 时跳过 preventDefault，使按键正常传递给终端。同时移除生产
环境的 reload/forceReload 菜单项以避免快捷键冲突。

Progressive per-league score loading — render each league as it arrives

Previously all 6 data sources (WBC, MLB, MiLB, NPB, KBO, NCAA) were
fetched in a single combined request, so the page waited for the slowest
source (often NPB/KBO scraping at 2-4s) before showing anything.

New architecture:
- Add 6 per-league API routes (/api/scores/wbc|mlb|milb|npb|kbo|ncaa)
- ScoresView fires all 6 fetches in parallel on mount/date change
- Each league slot shows a skeleton and renders the moment its data
  arrives — fast sources (WBC ~300ms, MLB ~600ms) appear first
- SSE live-stream unchanged: init event distributes data across slots
  on reconnect; update events still patch games by ID across all slots
- Pull-to-refresh resets all slots to loading and re-fires all fetches

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(security): resolve all GitHub security scan alerts (#1555)

## Summary

Fixes **14 open security alerts** across 4 categories:

### Critical/High
- **CVE-2026-22184** (CRITICAL, zlib) — portal + control-plane-ui Docker
images: added `apk upgrade --no-cache` in production stage
- **CVE-2026-27171** (MEDIUM, zlib) — same fix, same images
- **Insecure SSH HostKeyCallback** (HIGH, CodeQL #198) — hegemon/daemon:
replaced `ssh.InsecureIgnoreHostKey()` with `knownhosts.New()`,
configurable via `HEGEMON_KNOWN_HOSTS` env var (defaults to
`~/.ssh/known_hosts`)
- **minimatch ReDoS** (HIGH, Dependabot #48) — portal: added npm
`overrides` for `minimatch >=9.0.7`

### Medium (CodeQL workflow permissions)
- **#199** — stoa-operator-ci.yml: added `permissions: contents: read`
- **#186-192** (7 alerts) — promote-to-prod.yml: added `permissions:
contents: read, actions: read`

### Alerts resolved
| Alert | Severity | Tool | Status |
|-------|----------|------|--------|
| #200, #202 | CRITICAL | Trivy | Fixed (Dockerfile apk upgrade) |
| #201, #203 | MEDIUM | Trivy | Fixed (Dockerfile apk upgrade) |
| #198 | HIGH | CodeQL | Fixed (knownhosts) |
| #199 | MEDIUM | CodeQL | Fixed (permissions) |
| #186-192 | MEDIUM | CodeQL | Fixed (permissions) |
| Dep #48 | HIGH | Dependabot | Fixed (npm overrides) |

## Deployment note
After merge, the HEGEMON workers need `~/.ssh/known_hosts` populated
with the fleet host keys. Run on the deployment machine:
```bash
for host in <worker-ips>; do ssh-keyscan -H "$host" >> ~/.ssh/known_hosts; done
```
Or set `HEGEMON_KNOWN_HOSTS` env var to a custom path.

## Test plan
- [x] Go code compiles (`go build ./...`)
- [x] Go vet passes (`go vet ./...`)
- [x] Portal: `npm audit` shows 0 vulnerabilities
- [ ] CI green (3 required checks)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Fix upload wizard styling by enabling Tailwind CSS

PROBLEM DIAGNOSIS:
- Upload screens were completely unstyled (plain text, no formatting)
- User reported "horribly formatted and not very easy to understand"
- Components were using Tailwind classes but Tailwind CSS wasn't loading

ROOT CAUSE:
- index.css was missing @tailwind directives (@tailwind base/components/utilities)
- Tailwind CSS wasn't being injected into the build
- CSS file was only 2.20 kB instead of ~50 kB
- Global border-radius:0 !important was also breaking rounded corners

FIXES:
1. Added Tailwind directives to index.css
2. Removed global border-radius override from design-system.css
3. CSS now properly includes Tailwind (51.78 kB vs 2.20 kB)

RESULT:
- Upload wizard now shows properly styled UI with:
  - Visual step progress indicator with checkmarks
  - Clean card layouts with borders and shadows
  - Proper form styling with labels and validation
  - Color-coded help cards (blue/green/amber)
  - Professional buttons and spacing
  - Responsive grid layouts

COMPONENTS NOW PROPERLY STYLED:
- UploadWizard (step indicator, progress bar, cards)
- ProjectContextForm (form fields, validation, help text)
- DocumentUploadZone (drag-drop area, file list)
- DocumentValidationCard (validation results)

User experience improved from plain HTML to modern, professional UI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Implement remediation phases 0-11: critical fixes, ML hardening, performance optimization

Complete implementation of the Master Remediation Plan phases 0 through 11:
- Phase 0: Emergency fixes (NameError, race conditions, TOCTOU, connection leaks)
- Phase 1: ML pipeline integrity (EarlyStopping wiring, global seeds, train/val split, gradient clipping)
- Phase 2: Data layer hardening (path traversal, JSON size enforcement, thread-safe singletons)
- Phase 3: Processing correctness (PlayerRole unification, unit-of-measure fixes, role classification)
- Phase 4: UI/UX remediation (binding leaks, thread-safe messaging, MVVM, loading indicators)
- Phase 5: Dependency stabilization (KivyMD pinning, requirements cleanup)
- Phase 6: Test coverage & CI/CD (GitHub Actions 4-stage pipeline, regression tests)
- Phase 7: Security hardening (API key handling, rate limiting, path traversal protection)
- Phase 8: Heuristic validation (belief model calibration, documented constants)
- Phase 9: Architecture decisions (RAP Coach consolidation, experimental isolation)
- Phase 10: Documentation & packaging (Book-Coach docs, studies restructure)
- Phase 11: Performance optimization (vectorized heatmaps, game tree memoization,
  searchsorted flash correlation, pre-indexed player knowledge)

Headless validator: 294/298 PASS, 0 FAIL, 4 non-blocking warnings.
Includes Phase 11 review plan documenting test coverage gaps to address in Phase 12.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: return accumulated costs from commit_batch instead of discarding (#566)

PrefixedRocksDbImmediateStorageContext::commit_batch was using
Default::default() for its cost context, discarding all accumulated
costs (seek counts, storage costs) from the batch. Now returns
batch.cost_acc to preserve cost tracking accuracy.

commit.message:
fix: skip procedural geometry masks for 3D panels (#39)

* feat: maximize A1 sheet consistency with deterministic SVG pipeline and demo cherry-pick mode

Enable the canonical baseline pipeline so 2D technical panels (floor plans,
elevations, sections) are rendered as deterministic SVG from the BuildingModel
instead of being sent to FLUX AI. This ensures perfect geometric consistency
across all technical drawings.

Key changes:
- Enable useCanonicalBaseline + strictDeterministic2D feature flags
- Harden CDS-to-BuildingModel fallback with defensive massing defaults
- Switch SVG theme to "artistic" for presentation-quality drawings
- Add background rectangles and fallback window generation to SVGs
- Remove FLUX.1-schnell, use FLUX.1-dev (40 steps) everywhere
- Front-load material hex colors in hero_3d/interior_3d prompts
- Strengthen hero control strength for axonometric and elevation panels
- Warm paper background (#FAF9F6) for A1 composition
- Add panel regeneration UI with per-panel "Regenerate" buttons
- Add "Recompose A1 Sheet" button for cherry-picking best panels
- Add tier diagnostic logging (SVG vs FLUX panel routing)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add init_image fallback when Together.ai returns persistent 500 errors

When all 5 retry attempts fail with 500/503 and init_image was active
(geometry render or style reference), retry one final time WITHOUT
init_image. Together.ai sometimes rejects init_image payloads (large
SVGs, encoding issues) with 500 errors.

Also fixes:
- Float precision artifact on imageStrength (0.19999999999999996 → 0.2)
- Better error logging on first 500 to diagnose init_image issues

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: skip geometry SVG init_image for 3D panels to prevent Together.ai 500 errors

Together.ai FLUX API returns 500 when sent SVG data URLs as init_image
for 3D photorealistic panels (hero_3d, interior_3d, axonometric).
Skip geometry conditioning for these panel types — they generate from
prompt only with optional hero style reference.

Also includes init_image-removal fallback from previous commit for
defense in depth.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: skip procedural geometry masks for 3D panels and improve error messages

- Skip SVG procedural geometry masks for hero_3d, axonometric, interior_3d,
  and other 3D panel types in orchestrator PRIORITY 1 (same as canonical fix)
- Fix error message stringification: Together.ai nested error objects now
  display properly instead of [object Object]
- Verified: init_image no longer attached to 3D panels during generation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: replace closure-based health queue job with dedicated Job class

Closure-based dispatch was causing infinite loop — Laravel serializes
the closure source code including the dispatch() call, so each job
execution re-dispatched itself. Using a proper Job class eliminates this.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add RunContextWrapper type annotation to request_extension tool

The missing type annotation on ctx caused OpenAI API to reject the
tool schema ('ctx' had no 'type' key in JSON schema).

Fix Drive modal regression: use flex-basis auto instead of 0

flex-basis: 0 collapsed the content to zero height. Using auto
preserves content visibility while still allowing flex shrink
for proper scroll behavior.

commit.message:
fix: make all store functions async + use Redis-only storage for Vercel

- Add await to all store function calls across scripts and API routes
- Replace @vercel/blob with Redis-based blob storage (eliminates BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN requirement)
- Fix TypeScript errors in vercel storage cursor typing
- Remove @vercel/kv and @vercel/blob dependencies (only @upstash/redis needed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix CPU-only/Ollama-only: add router fallback to all direct micro-LLM calls

Root cause: consciousness_daemon, room_session, and pip_agent called
port 8105 (llama-server) directly, bypassing the router. On Ollama-only
setups (no llama-server), these calls fail with Connection refused,
killing all background thinking, room sessions, and the Pip agent.

Fix: wrap direct 8105 calls in try/except. On URLError/ConnectionRefused,
fall back to router /route with force="llama" — the router already has
full Ollama fallback since d179853.

Tested: simulated Ollama-only setup by stopping llama-servers.
- Router /route with force="llm" → ollama/qwen2.5:3b ✓
- Router /route with force="llama" → ollama/qwen2.5:3b ✓
- Router streaming → ollama/qwen2.5:3b ✓
- Direct 8105 → Connection refused (expected) ✓
- Fallback to router → works ✓

Also fixed pip_agent ROUTER_URL pointing to nonexistent
/v1/chat/completions on router (should be /route).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
